MASTER JURY ASBESTOS CALENDAR OF SEP-17-2012 CONTINUED TO SEP-26-2012 AND CONTINUED TO OCT-1-2012 ASSIGNED TO ASBESTOS JURY TRIAL CALENDAR ON OCT-03-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 613, ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH. JENNIFER ALESIO APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LISA OBERG APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT METALCLAD. THE COURT ASSIGNED THE CASE FOR TRIAL TO JUDGE GOLDSMITH SHORTLY AFTER AROUND 11:15AM AND DISCUSSED ABOUT JUDGE GOLDSMITH'S SCHEDULING. BETWEEN THE TIME OF THE TRIAL ASSIGNMENT BEING DONE AND 11:40AM, THE COURT HAD AN INFORMAL DISCUSSION WITH THE PARTIES PRESENT IN COURT REGARDING THE GENERAL ORDER AND CASE MANAGEMENT ORDER NOT PERTAINING TO THE TRIAL ASSIGNMENT. AS THE COURT IS ADJOURNED AND IS PREPARED TO LEAVE THE BENCH, PLAINTIFF REQUESTED IF SHE COULD BE SWORN TO EXERCISE A CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE GOLDSMITH. AT AROUND 11:45AM, THE COURT ALLOWED PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO BE SWORN BUT THE COURT FINDS THE CHALLENGE TO BE UNTIMELY. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED THAT IF COUNSEL BRINGS OR FINDS LEGAL AUTHORITY AS TO THE TIMELINESS OF THE CHALLENGE, THEN THE COURT WILL CONSIDER IT. PLAINTIFF WAS SWORN AND EXERCISED A 170.6 CCP PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE GOLDSMITH AS STATED ON THE RECORD. THE COURT WILL ENTERTAIN AT 3:00PM ON 10/2/12 IF PLAINTIFF FINDS LEGAL AUTHORITY ON THE CHALLENGE BEING TIMELY. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: KIMBERLY AVERY CSR#5074 (510-451-1580) (503)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2 (GENERAL NEGLIGENCE EXCLUDING NEGLIGENT FAILURE TO WARN, STRICT LIABILITY DESIGN AND MANUFACTURING DEFECT) AND DENIED AS TO ISSUE 3 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES). AS TO ISSUES 1 AND 2, DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE CASE THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO PREVAIL ON ITS GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE AS TO NON-DUTY TO WARN CLAIMS. THE COURT DID CONSIDER PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION AND THE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFFS DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE FOR THE LATE FILING AND SERVICE OF THEIR OPPOSITION AND ALL ATTACHED PAPERS. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THIS DEFENSE. (SEE HOBSON V. RAYCHEM CORP. (1999) 73 CAL.APP.4TH 614, 623-625 (DISAPPROVED ON OTHER GROUNDS IN COLMENARES V. BRAEMAR COUNTRY CLUB, INC. (2003) 29 CAL.4TH 1019).) AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT NOW POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AN ORDER AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: CAROL HARABURDA OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 8052

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Oct-01-2012 Assigned To Trial Court

Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'S Motion For Summary Adjudication (Part Two)
Off Calendar Sep-06-2012 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Gerald Sanders, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect) and denied as to issue 3 (punitive damages). As to issues 1 and 2, defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims. The Court did consider plaintiffs' opposition and the evidence. Plaintiffs demonstrated good cause for the late filing and service of their opposition and all attached papers. The court notes that plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Prevailing party to prepare an order and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Carol Haraburda of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 8052 (503/TLJ)
ASBESTOS SETTLEMENT CALENDAR

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jun-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Chris Hersom of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties
Alecia E. Cotton of MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4123), appearing for defendant / opposing party

RULING:
On the Court's own discretion, the Court will hear arguments on this motion even though two additional days for E-service was not provided by the moving party. Argued and the Court grants Plaintiff's Motion for Preference pursuant to CCP 36(d). The original trial date of 10/22/12 is vacated and this case is severed from Brayton Group 614. Trial date is 9/17/12 at 11:15 a.m. in Dept. 503. Parties stipulate to the last day for hearing summary motions is 9/6/12 and that summary motions may be heard on 15 days notice without an additional two days for e-service. Oppositions are due five court days before the hearing and replies due two court days before the hearing. Plaintiff is to make a settlement demand on all defendants no later than 8/30/12. Settlement conference is set in room 603 at 1:30pm on 9/6/12. Fact discovery cut-off date is 8/31. Expert discovery cut-off date is 9/14/12. No later than 6/29/12, plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than 6/29/12, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. The parties to meet and confer regarding any other scheduling or other issue which any of them want to have included in the preference order. Further case management conference is set on 8/29/12 at 1:30pm. In Dept. 503. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. Mandatory settlement conference with the court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 is set on 8/17/12 at 1:30 p.m. in Dept. 503. All clients are to be present unless permission is granted otherwise. Written order pending. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436, AIKEN WELCH COURT REPORTERS (510-451-1580) (503, TLJ)
